title: Your Body Language May Shape Who You Are | Amy Cuddy | TED

transcript.chinese: 译者：Joseph Geni 审稿人：Morton Bast
transcript.english: Translator: Joseph Geni Reviewer: Morton Bast

transcript.chinese: 因此，我想首先为您提供一个免费的无技术生活窍门，它要求您做的就是：改变姿势两分钟。
transcript.english: So I want to start by offering you a free no-tech life hack, and all it requires of you is this: that you change your posture for two minutes.

transcript.chinese: 但在我透露之前，我想请您现在对您的身体以及您对身体所做的事情进行一些检查。
transcript.english: But before I give it away, I want to ask you to right now do a little audit of your body and what you're doing with your body.

transcript.chinese: 那么，你们中有多少人在某种程度上让自己变得更小呢？
transcript.english: So how many of you are sort of making yourselves smaller?

transcript.chinese: 也许你弯腰驼背，交叉双腿，也许包裹着脚踝。
transcript.english: Maybe you're hunching, crossing your legs, maybe wrapping your ankles.

transcript.chinese: 有时我们会像这样握住手臂。
transcript.english: Sometimes we hold onto our arms like this.

transcript.chinese: 有时我们分散开来。 （笑声）我看到你了。
transcript.english: Sometimes we spread out. (Laughter) I see you.

transcript.chinese: 所以我希望你注意你现在正在做的事情。
transcript.english: So I want you to pay attention to what you're doing right now.

transcript.chinese: 我们将在几分钟后回到这个问题，我希望如果你学会稍微调整一下，它可以显着改变你的生活展开方式。
transcript.english: We're going to come back to that in a few minutes, and I'm hoping that if you learn to tweak this a little bit, it could significantly change the way your life unfolds.

transcript.chinese: 所以，我们真的对肢体语言着迷，而且对其他人的肢体语言特别感兴趣。
transcript.english: So, we're really fascinated with body language, and we're particularly interested in other people's body language.

transcript.chinese: 你知道，我们感兴趣的是，比如，你知道——（笑声）——尴尬的互动，或者一个微笑，或者一个轻蔑的眼神，或者一个非常尴尬的眨眼，或者甚至是握手之类的东西。
transcript.english: You know, we're interested in, like, you know — (Laughter) — an awkward interaction, or a smile, or a contemptuous glance, or maybe a very awkward wink, or maybe even something like a handshake.

transcript.chinese: 旁白：他们正在到达 10 号。
transcript.english: Narrator: Here they are arriving at Number 10.

transcript.chinese: 这位幸运的警察能够与美国总统握手。
transcript.english: This lucky policeman gets to shake hands with the President of the United States.

transcript.chinese: 总理来了——不。（笑声）（掌声）（笑声）（掌声） Amy Cuddy：所以一次握手，或者不握手，都会让我们聊上几周、几周、几周。
transcript.english: Here comes the Prime Minister -- No. (Laughter) (Applause) (Laughter) (Applause) Amy Cuddy: So a handshake, or the lack of a handshake, can have us talking for weeks and weeks and weeks.

transcript.chinese: 甚至 BBC 和纽约时报。
transcript.english: Even the BBC and The New York Times.

transcript.chinese: 显然，当我们考虑非语言行为或肢体语言时——但我们作为社会科学家称之为非语言——它是语言，所以我们考虑的是沟通。
transcript.english: So obviously when we think about nonverbal behavior, or body language -- but we call it nonverbals as social scientists -- it's language, so we think about communication.

transcript.chinese: 当我们考虑沟通时，我们会考虑互动。
transcript.english: When we think about communication, we think about interactions.

transcript.chinese: 那么你的肢体语言在向我传达什么信息呢？
transcript.english: So what is your body language communicating to me?

transcript.chinese: 我在跟你传达什么信息？
transcript.english: What's mine communicating to you?

transcript.chinese: 有很多理由相信这是看待这个问题的有效方法。
transcript.english: And there's a lot of reason to believe that this is a valid way to look at this.

transcript.chinese: 因此，社会科学家花了很多时间研究我们的肢体语言或其他人的肢体语言对判断的影响。
transcript.english: So social scientists have spent a lot of time looking at the effects of our body language, or other people's body language, on judgments.

transcript.chinese: 我们根据肢体语言做出全面的判断和推论。
transcript.english: And we make sweeping judgments and inferences from body language.

transcript.chinese: 这些判断可以预测真正有意义的生活结果，例如我们雇用或晋升谁，我们约谁出去。
transcript.english: And those judgments can predict really meaningful life outcomes like who we hire or promote, who we ask out on a date.

transcript.chinese: 例如，塔夫茨大学的研究员纳里尼·安巴迪（Nalini Ambady）表明，当人们观看 30 秒真实医患互动的无声片段时，他们对医生友善的判断可以预测该医生是否会被起诉。
transcript.english: For example, Nalini Ambady, a researcher at Tufts University, shows that when people watch 30-second soundless clips of real physician-patient interactions, their judgments of the physician's niceness predict whether or not that physician will be sued.

transcript.chinese: 因此，这与该医生是否无能没有太大关系，但我们是否喜欢那个人以及他们如何互动？
transcript.english: So it doesn't have to do so much with whether or not that physician was incompetent, but do we like that person and how they interacted?

transcript.chinese: 更戏剧性的是，普林斯顿大学的亚历克斯·托多罗夫（Alex Todorov）向我们展示了，只需一秒钟对政治候选人面孔的判断就能预测出美国 70% 的人
transcript.english: Even more dramatic, Alex Todorov at Princeton has shown us that judgments of political candidates' faces in just one second predict 70 percent of U.S.

transcript.chinese: 参议院和州长的竞选结果，甚至，让我们走向数字化，在线谈判中使用得当的表情符号可以让你从谈判中获得更多价值。
transcript.english: Senate and gubernatorial race outcomes, and even, let's go digital, emoticons used well in online negotiations can lead you to claim more value from that negotiation.

transcript.chinese: 如果你使用不当，那是个坏主意。
transcript.english: If you use them poorly, bad idea.

transcript.chinese: 正确的？
transcript.english: Right?

transcript.chinese: 因此，当我们想到非语言时，我们会想到我们如何评价他人，他们如何评价我们以及结果是什么。
transcript.english: So when we think of nonverbals, we think of how we judge others, how they judge us and what the outcomes are.

transcript.chinese: 然而，我们往往会忘记受我们的非语言影响的其他观众，那就是我们自己。
transcript.english: We tend to forget, though, the other audience that's influenced by our nonverbals, and that's ourselves.

transcript.chinese: 我们还受到我们的非语言、我们的思想、我们的感受和我们的生理的影响。
transcript.english: We are also influenced by our nonverbals, our thoughts and our feelings and our physiology.

transcript.chinese: 那么我在谈论什么非语言呢？
transcript.english: So what nonverbals am I talking about?

transcript.chinese: 我是一名社会心理学家。
transcript.english: I'm a social psychologist.

transcript.chinese: 我研究偏见，在一所竞争激烈的商学院任教，所以我不可避免地会对权力动态产生兴趣。
transcript.english: I study prejudice, and I teach at a competitive business school, so it was inevitable that I would become interested in power dynamics.

transcript.chinese: 我对权力和统治力的非语言表达特别感兴趣。
transcript.english: I became especially interested in nonverbal expressions of power and dominance.

transcript.chinese: 什么是权力和统治力的非语言表达？
transcript.english: And what are nonverbal expressions of power and dominance?

transcript.chinese: 嗯，这就是他们的本质。
transcript.english: Well, this is what they are.

transcript.chinese: 所以在动物王国里，它们是在扩张。
transcript.english: So in the animal kingdom, they are about expanding.

transcript.chinese: 所以你让自己变得更大，你伸展，你占据空间，你基本上是在开放。
transcript.english: So you make yourself big, you stretch out, you take up space, you're basically opening up.

transcript.chinese: 这是关于开放。
transcript.english: It's about opening up.

transcript.chinese: 在整个动物王国都是如此。
transcript.english: And this is true across the animal kingdom.

transcript.chinese: 它不仅限于灵长类动物。
transcript.english: It's not just limited to primates.

transcript.chinese: 人类也做同样的事情。 （笑声）所以，当他们长期拥有权力时，以及当他们当下感觉强大时，他们都会这样做。
transcript.english: And humans do the same thing. (Laughter) So they do this both when they have power sort of chronically, and also when they're feeling powerful in the moment.

transcript.chinese: 这一点特别有趣，因为它确实向我们展示了这些权力表达方式是多么普遍和古老。
transcript.english: And this one is especially interesting because it really shows us how universal and old these expressions of power are.

transcript.chinese: 杰西卡·特蕾西研究过这种被称为骄傲的表达方式。
transcript.english: This expression, which is known as pride, Jessica Tracy has studied.

transcript.chinese: 她表明，天生有视力的人和先天失明的人在体育比赛中获胜时都会这样做。
transcript.english: She shows that people who are born with sight and people who are congenitally blind do this when they win at a physical competition.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当他们冲过终点线并获胜时，即使他们从未见过有人这样做也没关系。
transcript.english: So when they cross the finish line and they've won, it doesn't matter if they've never seen anyone do it.

transcript.chinese: 他们这样做。
transcript.english: They do this.

transcript.chinese: 所以双臂向上呈V字形，下巴微微抬起。
transcript.english: So the arms up in the V, the chin is slightly lifted.

transcript.chinese: 当我们感到无能为力时，我们该怎么办？
transcript.english: What do we do when we feel powerless?

transcript.chinese: 我们的做法恰恰相反。
transcript.english: We do exactly the opposite.

transcript.chinese: 我们关闭。
transcript.english: We close up.

transcript.chinese: 我们把自己包裹起来。
transcript.english: We wrap ourselves up.

transcript.chinese: 我们让自己变得渺小。
transcript.english: We make ourselves small.

transcript.chinese: 我们不想撞到旁边的人。
transcript.english: We don't want to bump into the person next to us.

transcript.chinese: 同样，动物和人类都做同样的事情。
transcript.english: So again, both animals and humans do the same thing.

transcript.chinese: 这就是当你将高功率和低功率放在一起时会发生的情况。
transcript.english: And this is what happens when you put together high and low power.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当涉及到权力时，我们倾向于做的就是补充对方的非语言能力。
transcript.english: So what we tend to do when it comes to power is that we complement the other's nonverbals.

transcript.chinese: 因此，如果有人对我们非常强大，我们就会倾向于让自己变得更渺小。
transcript.english: So if someone is being really powerful with us, we tend to make ourselves smaller.

transcript.chinese: 我们不镜像他们。
transcript.english: We don't mirror them.

transcript.chinese: 我们做的与他们相反。
transcript.english: We do the opposite of them.

transcript.chinese: 那么我在教室里观察这种行为，我注意到了什么？
transcript.english: So I'm watching this behavior in the classroom, and what do I notice?

transcript.chinese: 我注意到 MBA 学生确实展现出了全方位的非语言能力。
transcript.english: I notice that MBA students really exhibit the full range of power nonverbals.

transcript.chinese: 所以有些人就像漫画中的阿尔法，真正进入房间，他们甚至在上课之前就进入房间的中间，就像他们真的想占据空间一样。
transcript.english: So you have people who are like caricatures of alphas, really coming into the room, they get right into the middle of the room before class even starts, like they really want to occupy space.

transcript.chinese: 当他们坐下时，他们会分散开来。
transcript.english: When they sit down, they're sort of spread out.

transcript.chinese: 他们像这样举起手来。
transcript.english: They raise their hands like this.

transcript.chinese: 还有其他人进来时几乎崩溃了。
transcript.english: You have other people who are virtually collapsing when they come in.

transcript.chinese: 他们一进来你就看到了。
transcript.english: As soon they come in, you see it.

transcript.chinese: 你可以在他们的脸上和身体上看到这一点，他们坐在椅子上，把自己变得很小，当他们举起手时，他们就是这样。
transcript.english: You see it on their faces and their bodies, and they sit in their chair and they make themselves tiny, and they go like this when they raise their hand.

transcript.chinese: 我注意到有关于此的一些事情。
transcript.english: I notice a couple of things about this.

transcript.chinese: 第一，你不会感到惊讶。
transcript.english: One, you're not going to be surprised.

transcript.chinese: 这似乎与性别有关。
transcript.english: It seems to be related to gender.

transcript.chinese: 所以女性比男性更容易做这种事情。
transcript.english: So women are much more likely to do this kind of thing than men.

transcript.chinese: 女性长期感觉自己不如男性强大，所以这并不奇怪。
transcript.english: Women feel chronically less powerful than men, so this is not surprising.

transcript.chinese: 但我注意到的另一件事是，这似乎也与学生参与的程度以及参与的程度有关。
transcript.english: But the other thing I noticed is that it also seemed to be related to the extent to which the students were participating, and how well they were participating.

transcript.chinese: 这在 MBA 课堂上非常重要，因为参与度占成绩的一半。
transcript.english: And this is really important in the MBA classroom, because participation counts for half the grade.

transcript.chinese: 因此，商学院一直在努力解决这种性别成绩差距。
transcript.english: So business schools have been struggling with this gender grade gap.

transcript.chinese: 你让这些同等资格的女性和男性进来，然后你就会发现成绩上的差异，这似乎部分归因于参与。
transcript.english: You get these equally qualified women and men coming in and then you get these differences in grades, and it seems to be partly attributable to participation.

transcript.chinese: 所以我开始想，你知道，好吧，所以你让这些人像这样进来，他们正在参与。
transcript.english: So I started to wonder, you know, okay, so you have these people coming in like this, and they're participating.

transcript.chinese: 我们是否有可能让人们伪造它并会导致他们更多地参与？
transcript.english: Is it possible that we could get people to fake it and would it lead them to participate more?

transcript.chinese: 所以我的主要合作者达纳·卡尼（Dana Carney）在伯克利大学，我真的很想知道，你能假装它直到你成功吗？
transcript.english: So my main collaborator Dana Carney, who's at Berkeley, and I really wanted to know, can you fake it till you make it?

transcript.chinese: 比如，你能这样做一段时间，并真正体验到让你看起来更强大的行为结果吗？
transcript.english: Like, can you do this just for a little while and actually experience a behavioral outcome that makes you seem more powerful?

transcript.chinese: 所以我们知道，我们的非语言行为决定着其他人对我们的看法和感受。
transcript.english: So we know that our nonverbals govern how other people think and feel about us.

transcript.chinese: 有很多证据。
transcript.english: There's a lot of evidence.

transcript.chinese: 但我们真正的问题是，我们的非语言行为是否决定了我们对自己的思考和感受？
transcript.english: But our question really was, do our nonverbals govern how we think and feel about ourselves?

transcript.chinese: 有一些证据表明他们确实这样做。
transcript.english: There's some evidence that they do.

transcript.chinese: 所以，举例来说，当我们感到快乐时，我们会微笑，而且，当我们像这样用牙齿叼着一支笔被迫微笑时，也会让我们感到快乐。
transcript.english: So, for example, we smile when we feel happy, but also, when we're forced to smile by holding a pen in our teeth like this, it makes us feel happy.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是双向的。
transcript.english: So it goes both ways.

transcript.chinese: 当谈到权力时，它也是双向的。
transcript.english: When it comes to power, it also goes both ways.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当你感觉自己很强大时，你就更有可能这样做，但也有可能当你假装很强大时，你更有可能真正感到自己很强大。
transcript.english: So when you feel powerful, you're more likely to do this, but it's also possible that when you pretend to be powerful, you are more likely to actually feel powerful.

transcript.chinese: 所以第二个问题确实是，你知道，所以我们知道我们的思想改变我们的身体，但是我们的身体也改变我们的思想吗？
transcript.english: So the second question really was, you know, so we know that our minds change our bodies, but is it also true that our bodies change our minds?

transcript.chinese: 当我说思想时，就强者而言，我在说什么？
transcript.english: And when I say minds, in the case of the powerful, what am I talking about?

transcript.chinese: 所以我谈论的是想法和感受，以及构成我们的想法和感受的生理因素，就我而言，这就是荷尔蒙。
transcript.english: So I'm talking about thoughts and feelings and the sort of physiological things that make up our thoughts and feelings, and in my case, that's hormones.

transcript.chinese: 我看荷尔蒙。
transcript.english: I look at hormones.

transcript.chinese: 那么，强者与弱者的思想是什么样的呢？
transcript.english: So what do the minds of the powerful versus the powerless look like?

transcript.chinese: 毫不奇怪，如此强大的人往往会更加自信、更加自信、更加乐观。
transcript.english: So powerful people tend to be, not surprisingly, more assertive and more confident, more optimistic.

transcript.chinese: 他们实际上觉得即使在机会游戏中他们也会获胜。
transcript.english: They actually feel they're going to win even at games of chance.

transcript.chinese: 他们也往往能够进行更抽象的思考。
transcript.english: They also tend to be able to think more abstractly.

transcript.chinese: 所以有很多差异。
transcript.english: So there are a lot of differences.

transcript.chinese: 他们承担更多的风险。
transcript.english: They take more risks.

transcript.chinese: 有权力的人和无权力的人之间有很多区别。
transcript.english: There are a lot of differences between powerful and powerless people.

transcript.chinese: 从生理学角度来看，两种关键激素也存在差异：睾酮（主导激素）和皮质醇（应激激素）。
transcript.english: Physiologically, there also are differences on two key hormones: testosterone, which is the dominance hormone, and cortisol, which is the stress hormone.

transcript.chinese: 所以我们发现，灵长类动物等级中的高权力雄性领袖具有高睾酮和低皮质醇，而强大而有效的领导者也具有高睾酮和低皮质醇。
transcript.english: So what we find is that high-power alpha males in primate hierarchies have high testosterone and low cortisol, and powerful and effective leaders also have high testosterone and low cortisol.

transcript.chinese: 那么这意味着什么呢？
transcript.english: So what does that mean?

transcript.chinese: 当你想到权力时，人们往往只想到睾丸激素，因为那意味着统治地位。
transcript.english: When you think about power, people tended to think only about testosterone, because that was about dominance.

transcript.chinese: 但实际上，力量还在于你如何应对压力。
transcript.english: But really, power is also about how you react to stress.

transcript.chinese: 那么，你想要一个高权力的领导者吗？他具有主导地位，睾丸激素水平高，但对压力非常敏感？
transcript.english: So do you want the high-power leader that's dominant, high on testosterone, but really stress reactive?

transcript.chinese: 可能不会，对吧？
transcript.english: Probably not, right?

transcript.chinese: 你想要的是一个有力量、自信、有统治力的人，但又不太容易受到压力影响，是一个悠闲的人。
transcript.english: You want the person who's powerful and assertive and dominant, but not very stress reactive, the person who's laid back.

transcript.chinese: 所以我们知道，在灵长类等级制度中，如果一个阿尔法需要接管，如果一个个体需要突然接管阿尔法角色，在几天之内，该个体的睾丸激素会显着上升，而他的皮质醇会显着下降。
transcript.english: So we know that in primate hierarchies, if an alpha needs to take over, if an individual needs to take over an alpha role sort of suddenly, within a few days, that individual's testosterone has gone up significantly and his cortisol has dropped significantly.

transcript.chinese: 所以我们有证据表明，身体可以塑造心灵，至少在面部层面，而且角色的变化也可以塑造心灵。
transcript.english: So we have this evidence, both that the body can shape the mind, at least at the facial level, and also that role changes can shape the mind.

transcript.chinese: 那么会发生什么，好吧，你进行角色改变，如果你在一个非常小的水平上这样做，比如这种微小的操纵，这种微小的干预，会发生什么？
transcript.english: So what happens, okay, you take a role change, what happens if you do that at a really minimal level, like this tiny manipulation, this tiny intervention?

transcript.chinese: “两分钟，”你说，“我希望你像这样站着，这会让你感觉更有力量。”这就是我们所做的。
transcript.english: "For two minutes," you say, "I want you to stand like this, and it's going to make you feel more powerful." So this is what we did.

transcript.chinese: 我们决定让人们进入实验室并进行一个小实验，这些人在两分钟内采用了高功率姿势或低功率姿势，我将向您展示其中五个姿势，尽管他们只采取了两个姿势。
transcript.english: We decided to bring people into the lab and run a little experiment, and these people adopted, for two minutes, either high-power poses or low-power poses, and I'm just going to show you five of the poses, although they took on only two.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是一个。
transcript.english: So here's one.

transcript.chinese: 还有几个。
transcript.english: A couple more.

transcript.chinese: 这位被媒体称为“神奇女侠”的人。
transcript.english: This one has been dubbed the "Wonder Woman" by the media.

transcript.chinese: 这里还有一些。
transcript.english: Here are a couple more.

transcript.chinese: 所以你可以站着，也可以坐着。
transcript.english: So you can be standing or you can be sitting.

transcript.chinese: 这是低功率姿势。
transcript.english: And here are the low-power poses.

transcript.chinese: 所以你折叠起来，你让自己变小。
transcript.english: So you're folding up, you're making yourself small.

transcript.chinese: 这个功率非常低。
transcript.english: This one is very low-power.

transcript.chinese: 当你触摸自己的脖子时，你实际上是在保护自己。
transcript.english: When you're touching your neck, you're really protecting yourself.

transcript.chinese: 所以这就是发生的事情。
transcript.english: So this is what happens.

transcript.chinese: 他们进来，向小瓶吐口水，持续两分钟，我们说，“你需要这样做或这样做。”他们不看姿势照片。
transcript.english: They come in, they spit into a vial, for two minutes, we say, "You need to do this or this." They don't look at pictures of the poses.

transcript.chinese: 我们不想让他们灌输权力概念。
transcript.english: We don't want to prime them with a concept of power.

transcript.chinese: 我们希望他们感受到力量。
transcript.english: We want them to be feeling power.

transcript.chinese: 他们这样做了两分钟。
transcript.english: So two minutes they do this.

transcript.chinese: 然后我们问他们：“你觉得自己有多强大？”在一系列项目上，然后我们给他们一个赌博的机会，然后我们又采集了另一个唾液样本。
transcript.english: We then ask them, "How powerful do you feel?" on a series of items, and then we give them an opportunity to gamble, and then we take another saliva sample.

transcript.chinese: 就是这样。
transcript.english: That's it.

transcript.chinese: 这就是整个实验。
transcript.english: That's the whole experiment.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我们发现的。
transcript.english: So this is what we find.

transcript.chinese: 风险承受能力，也就是赌博，我们发现当你处于高权力姿势的时候，86%的人会赌博。
transcript.english: Risk tolerance, which is the gambling, we find that when you are in the high-power pose condition, 86 percent of you will gamble.

transcript.chinese: 当你处于低功率姿势状态时，只有 60%，这是一个巨大的显着差异。
transcript.english: When you're in the low-power pose condition, only 60 percent, and that's a whopping significant difference.

transcript.chinese: 这是我们在睾酮方面的发现。
transcript.english: Here's what we find on testosterone.

transcript.chinese: 从他们进来时的基线来看，高权力的人经历了大约 20% 的增长，而低权力的人经历了大约 10% 的下降。
transcript.english: From their baseline when they come in, high-power people experience about a 20-percent increase, and low-power people experience about a 10-percent decrease.

transcript.chinese: 再说一遍，两分钟，你就会得到这些变化。
transcript.english: So again, two minutes, and you get these changes.

transcript.chinese: 这就是你从皮质醇中得到的结果。
transcript.english: Here's what you get on cortisol.

transcript.chinese: 高权力的人经历了大约 25% 的减少，而低权力的人经历了大约 15% 的增加。
transcript.english: High-power people experience about a 25-percent decrease, and the low-power people experience about a 15-percent increase.

transcript.chinese: 因此，两分钟会导致荷尔蒙发生变化，使您的大脑基本上变得自信、自信和舒适，或者真正对压力敏感，感觉有点封闭。
transcript.english: So two minutes lead to these hormonal changes that configure your brain to basically be either assertive, confident and comfortable, or really stress-reactive, and feeling sort of shut down.

transcript.chinese: 我们都有过这种感觉，对吧？
transcript.english: And we've all had the feeling, right?

transcript.chinese: 所以看来我们的非语言确实控制着我们对自己的思考和感受，所以这不仅是别人的，也是我们自己的。
transcript.english: So it seems that our nonverbals do govern how we think and feel about ourselves, so it's not just others, but it's also ourselves.

transcript.chinese: 此外，我们的身体会改变我们的想法。
transcript.english: Also, our bodies change our minds.

transcript.chinese: 但下一个问题当然是，几分钟的强力姿势真的能以有意义的方式改变你的生活吗？
transcript.english: But the next question, of course, is, can power posing for a few minutes really change your life in meaningful ways?

transcript.chinese: 这是在实验室里，这是一个小任务，只需几分钟。
transcript.english: This is in the lab, it's this little task, it's just a couple of minutes.

transcript.chinese: 你可以在哪里实际应用这个？
transcript.english: Where can you actually apply this?

transcript.chinese: 当然，我们关心这一点。
transcript.english: Which we cared about, of course.

transcript.chinese: 因此，我们认为您想要在评估情况下使用它，例如社会威胁情况。
transcript.english: And so we think where you want to use this is evaluative situations, like social threat situations.

transcript.chinese: 您的朋友在哪里对您进行评价？
transcript.english: Where are you being evaluated, either by your friends?

transcript.chinese: 对于青少年来说，它是在午餐桌上。
transcript.english: For teenagers, it's at the lunchroom table.

transcript.chinese: 对于某些人来说，这是在学校董事会会议上的讲话。
transcript.english: For some people it's speaking at a school board meeting.

transcript.chinese: 它可能是进行推销或进行这样的演讲或进行工作面试。
transcript.english: It might be giving a pitch or giving a talk like this or doing a job interview.

transcript.chinese: 我们决定，大多数人都可以理解的面试就是工作面试，因为大多数人都经历过。
transcript.english: We decided that the one that most people could relate to because most people had been through, was the job interview.

transcript.chinese: 所以我们发表了这些发现，媒体都在关注它，他们说，好吧，这就是你参加工作面试时要做的，对吧？
transcript.english: So we published these findings, and the media are all over it, and they say, Okay, so this is what you do when you go in for the job interview, right?

transcript.chinese: （笑声）你知道，所以我们当然感到震惊，并说，天啊，不，我们根本不是这个意思。
transcript.english: (Laughter) You know, so we were of course horrified, and said, Oh my God, no, that's not what we meant at all.

transcript.chinese: 由于多种原因，不，不要这样做。
transcript.english: For numerous reasons, no, don't do that.

transcript.chinese: 再说一遍，这不是你与其他人交谈的问题。
transcript.english: Again, this is not about you talking to other people.

transcript.chinese: 这是你在自言自语。
transcript.english: It's you talking to yourself.

transcript.chinese: 在参加工作面试之前你会做什么？
transcript.english: What do you do before you go into a job interview?

transcript.chinese: 你这样做。
transcript.english: You do this.

transcript.chinese: 你坐下了。
transcript.english: You're sitting down.

transcript.chinese: 您正在查看您的 iPhone 或 Android，而不是试图将任何人排除在外。
transcript.english: You're looking at your iPhone -- or your Android, not trying to leave anyone out.

transcript.chinese: 你正在看你的笔记，你弯腰驼背，让自己变得渺小，而你真正应该做的也许是这样，比如，在浴室里，对吧？
transcript.english: You're looking at your notes, you're hunching up, making yourself small, when really what you should be doing maybe is this, like, in the bathroom, right?

transcript.chinese: 这样做。
transcript.english: Do that.

transcript.chinese: 找两分钟。
transcript.english: Find two minutes.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我们想要测试的。
transcript.english: So that's what we want to test.

transcript.chinese: 好的？
transcript.english: Okay?

transcript.chinese: 所以我们把人们带进实验室，他们再次摆出高权力或低权力的姿势，他们经历了非常紧张的工作面试。
transcript.english: So we bring people into a lab, and they do either high- or low-power poses again, they go through a very stressful job interview.

transcript.chinese: 有五分钟长。
transcript.english: It's five minutes long.

transcript.chinese: 他们正在被记录。
transcript.english: They are being recorded.

transcript.chinese: 他们也在接受评判，评委们接受过不提供非语言反馈的培训，所以他们看起来像这样。
transcript.english: They're being judged also, and the judges are trained to give no nonverbal feedback, so they look like this.

transcript.chinese: 想象一下这是面试你的人。
transcript.english: Imagine this is the person interviewing you.

transcript.chinese: 五分钟之内，什么也没有，这比被质问更糟糕。
transcript.english: So for five minutes, nothing, and this is worse than being heckled.

transcript.chinese: 人们讨厌这个。
transcript.english: People hate this.

transcript.chinese: 这就是玛丽安·拉弗朗斯所说的“站在社会流沙中”。所以这确实会刺激你的皮质醇。
transcript.english: It's what Marianne LaFrance calls "standing in social quicksand." So this really spikes your cortisol.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我们对他们进行的面试，因为我们真的想看看发生了什么。
transcript.english: So this is the job interview we put them through, because we really wanted to see what happened.

transcript.chinese: 然后我们让这些编码员查看这些磁带，共四盘。
transcript.english: We then have these coders look at these tapes, four of them.

transcript.chinese: 他们对这个假设视而不见。
transcript.english: They're blind to the hypothesis.

transcript.chinese: 他们对条件视而不见。
transcript.english: They're blind to the conditions.

transcript.chinese: 他们不知道谁摆出了什么姿势，最后他们看了这些录像带，然后说，“我们想雇用这些人”，所有这些人都是高权力的摆姿势者。
transcript.english: They have no idea who's been posing in what pose, and they end up looking at these sets of tapes, and they say, "We want to hire these people," all the high-power posers.

transcript.chinese: “我们不想雇用这些人。
transcript.english: "We don't want to hire these people.

transcript.chinese: 我们对这些人的总体评价也更加积极。”但是什么推动了它呢？
transcript.english: We also evaluate these people much more positively overall." But what's driving it?

transcript.chinese: 这与演讲的内容无关。
transcript.english: It's not about the content of the speech.

transcript.chinese: 这是关于他们在演讲中的表现。
transcript.english: It's about the presence that they're bringing to the speech.

transcript.chinese: 因为我们根据与能力相关的所有这些变量对他们进行评分，例如演讲的结构如何？
transcript.english: Because we rate them on all these variables related to competence, like, how well-structured is the speech?

transcript.chinese: 有多好？
transcript.english: How good is it?

transcript.chinese: 他们有什么资格？
transcript.english: What are their qualifications?

transcript.chinese: 对那些东西没有影响。
transcript.english: No effect on those things.

transcript.chinese: 这就是受到影响的。
transcript.english: This is what's affected.

transcript.chinese: 诸如此类的事情。
transcript.english: These kinds of things.

transcript.chinese: 基本上，人们正在展现真实的自己。
transcript.english: People are bringing their true selves, basically.

transcript.chinese: 他们自己带来。
transcript.english: They're bringing themselves.

transcript.chinese: 他们带来了自己的想法，但就像他们自己一样，你知道，没有任何残留。
transcript.english: They bring their ideas, but as themselves, with no, you know, residue over them.

transcript.chinese: 所以这就是驱动效应或调节效应的因素。
transcript.english: So this is what's driving the effect, or mediating the effect.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当我告诉人们这一点时，我们的身体改变我们的思想，我们的思想可以改变我们的行为，我们的行为可以改变我们的结果，他们对我说，“这感觉很假。”正确的？
transcript.english: So when I tell people about this, that our bodies change our minds and our minds can change our behavior, and our behavior can change our outcomes, they say to me, "It feels fake." Right?

transcript.chinese: 所以我说，假装它直到你成功为止。
transcript.english: So I said, fake it till you make it.

transcript.chinese: 这不是我。
transcript.english: It's not me.

transcript.chinese: 我不想到了那里之后仍然感觉自己像个骗子。
transcript.english: I don't want to get there and then still feel like a fraud.

transcript.chinese: 我不想感觉自己像个骗子。
transcript.english: I don't want to feel like an impostor.

transcript.chinese: 我不想到了那里只是觉得我不应该在这里。
transcript.english: I don't want to get there only to feel like I'm not supposed to be here.

transcript.chinese: 这确实引起了我的共鸣，因为我想告诉你一个关于作为冒名顶替者的小故事，并且感觉我不应该在这里。
transcript.english: And that really resonated with me, because I want to tell you a little story about being an impostor and feeling like I'm not supposed to be here.

transcript.chinese: 当我19岁的时候，我遭遇了一场严重的车祸。
transcript.english: When I was 19, I was in a really bad car accident.

transcript.chinese: 我被从车里扔了出来，滚了好几圈。
transcript.english: I was thrown out of a car, rolled several times.

transcript.chinese: 我被从车上扔了出来。
transcript.english: I was thrown from the car.

transcript.chinese: 我在头部受伤康复病房醒来，我已经从大学退学，我得知我的智商下降了两个标准差，这是非常痛苦的。
transcript.english: And I woke up in a head injury rehab ward, and I had been withdrawn from college, and I learned that my IQ had dropped by two standard deviations, which was very traumatic.

transcript.chinese: 我知道自己的智商，因为我认为自己很聪明，小时候就被称为天才。
transcript.english: I knew my IQ because I had identified with being smart, and I had been called gifted as a child.

transcript.chinese: 所以我被大学退学了，我一直在努力回去。
transcript.english: So I'm taken out of college, I keep trying to go back.

transcript.chinese: 他们说，“你不会完成大学学业。
transcript.english: They say, "You're not going to finish college.

transcript.chinese: 只是，你知道，你还有其他事情要做，但这对你来说是行不通的。”所以我真的很挣扎，我不得不说，你的身份被剥夺了，你的核心身份被剥夺了，对我来说这是明智的，失去了你的身份，没有什么比这更让你感到无能为力了。
transcript.english: Just, you know, there are other things for you to do, but that's not going to work out for you." So I really struggled with this, and I have to say, having your identity taken from you, your core identity, and for me it was being smart, having that taken from you, there's nothing that leaves you feeling more powerless than that.

transcript.chinese: 所以我感到完全无能为力。
transcript.english: So I felt entirely powerless.

transcript.chinese: 我工作又工作，我很幸运，我工作，我很幸运，我工作。
transcript.english: I worked and worked, and I got lucky, and worked, and got lucky, and worked.

transcript.chinese: 最终我大学毕业了。
transcript.english: Eventually I graduated from college.

transcript.chinese: 我比同龄人多花了四年时间，然后我说服了一个人，我的天使顾问苏珊·菲斯克（Susan Fiske）来接受我，所以我最终来到了普林斯顿大学，我当时想，我不应该在这里。
transcript.english: It took me four years longer than my peers, and I convinced someone, my angel advisor, Susan Fiske, to take me on, and so I ended up at Princeton, and I was like, I am not supposed to be here.

transcript.chinese: 我是个骗子。
transcript.english: I am an impostor.

transcript.chinese: 在我第一年演讲的前一天晚上，以及在普林斯顿大学的第一年演讲是对 20 个人进行 20 分钟的演讲。
transcript.english: And the night before my first-year talk, and the first-year talk at Princeton is a 20-minute talk to 20 people.

transcript.chinese: 就是这样。
transcript.english: That's it.

transcript.chinese: 我很害怕第二天被发现，所以我打电话给她说：“我要辞职了。”她就像是，“你不会放弃，因为我在你身上下了赌注，而你要留下来。
transcript.english: I was so afraid of being found out the next day that I called her and said, "I'm quitting." She was like, "You are not quitting, because I took a gamble on you, and you're staying.

transcript.chinese: 你要留下来，这就是你要做的事。
transcript.english: You're going to stay, and this is what you're going to do.

transcript.chinese: 你要假装它。
transcript.english: You are going to fake it.

transcript.chinese: 你要去做你被要求做的每一个演讲。
transcript.english: You're going to do every talk that you ever get asked to do.

transcript.chinese: 你只是要做它，做它，做它，即使你很害怕，只是瘫痪并有一个灵魂出窍的体验，直到你说，‘哦，天哪，我正在这么做，我已经变成这样了。’”这就是我所做的。
transcript.english: You're just going to do it and do it and do it, even if you're terrified and just paralyzed and having an out-of-body experience, until you have this moment where you say, 'Oh my gosh, I'm doing it.

transcript.chinese: 在研究生院读了五年，几年，你知道，我在西北大学，我搬到了哈佛，我在哈佛，我不再真正考虑它了，但很长一段时间我一直在想，“不应该在这里。”因此，在我在哈佛的第一年结束时，一个整个学期都没有在课堂上讲话的学生，
transcript.english: Like, I have become this.

transcript.chinese: 我曾说过，“看，你必须参与，否则你就会失败”，他走进了我的办公室。
transcript.english: I am actually doing this.'" So that's what I did.

transcript.chinese: 我真的根本不认识她。
transcript.english: Five years in grad school, a few years, you know, I'm at Northwestern, I moved to Harvard, I'm at Harvard, I'm not really thinking about it anymore, but for a long time I had been thinking, "Not supposed to be here." So at the end of my first year at Harvard, a student who had not talked in class the entire semester,

transcript.chinese: 她完全失败了，她说：“我不应该在这里。”
transcript.english: who I had said, "Look, you've gotta participate or else you're going to fail," came into my office.

transcript.chinese: 那就是我的时刻。
transcript.english: I really didn't know her at all.

transcript.chinese: 因为发生了两件事。
transcript.english: She came in totally defeated, and she said, "I'm not supposed to be here."

transcript.chinese: 一是我意识到，天哪，我不再有那样的感觉了。
transcript.english: And that was the moment for me.

transcript.chinese: 我不再有这种感觉了，但她有，而且我有这种感觉。
transcript.english: Because two things happened.

transcript.chinese: 第二个是，她应该在这里！
transcript.english: One was that I realized, oh my gosh, I don't feel like that anymore.

transcript.chinese: 就像，她可以假装它，她可以成为它。
transcript.english: I don't feel that anymore, but she does, and I get that feeling.

transcript.chinese: 所以我说，“是的，你是！
transcript.english: And the second was, she is supposed to be here!

transcript.chinese: 你应该在这里！
transcript.english: Like, she can fake it, she can become it.

transcript.chinese: 明天你要假装它，你要让自己变得强大，而且，你知道——（掌声）你要走进教室，你要给出有史以来最好的评论。”你知道？
transcript.english: So I was like, "Yes, you are!

transcript.chinese: 她给出了有史以来最好的评论，人们转过身来，说，天哪，我什至没有注意到她坐在那里。 （笑声）
transcript.english: You are supposed to be here!

transcript.chinese: 几个月后她回到我身边，我意识到她不仅仅是假装成功，而是实际上假装成功。
transcript.english: And tomorrow you're going to fake it, you're going to make yourself powerful, and, you know -- (Applause) And you're going to go into the classroom, and you are going to give the best comment ever." You know?

transcript.chinese: 所以她变了。
transcript.english: And she gave the best comment ever, and people turned around and were like, oh my God, I didn't even notice her sitting there. (Laughter)

transcript.chinese: 所以我想对你说，在你成功之前不要假装它。
transcript.english: She comes back to me months later, and I realized that she had not just faked it till she made it, she had actually faked it till she became it.

transcript.chinese: 假装它直到你成为它。
transcript.english: So she had changed.

transcript.chinese: 做得足够多，直到你真正成为它并内化为止。
transcript.english: And so I want to say to you, don't fake it till you make it.

transcript.chinese: 我要留给你的最后一件事就是这个。
transcript.english: Fake it till you become it.

transcript.chinese: 微小的调整可能会带来巨大的变化。
transcript.english: Do it enough until you actually become it and internalize.

transcript.chinese: 所以，这是两分钟。
transcript.english: The last thing I'm going to leave you with is this.

transcript.chinese: 两分钟，两分钟，两分钟。
transcript.english: Tiny tweaks can lead to big changes.

transcript.chinese: 在你进入下一个有压力的评估情境之前，花两分钟时间，在电梯里、在浴室隔间里、在紧闭门后的办公桌前尝试这样做。
transcript.english: So, this is two minutes.

transcript.chinese: 这就是你想做的。
transcript.english: Two minutes, two minutes, two minutes.

transcript.chinese: 配置你的大脑以在这种情况下最好地应对。
transcript.english: Before you go into the next stressful evaluative situation, for two minutes, try doing this, in the elevator, in a bathroom stall, at your desk behind closed doors.

transcript.chinese: 让你的睾丸激素升高。
transcript.english: That's what you want to do.

transcript.chinese: 让你的皮质醇下降。
transcript.english: Configure your brain to cope the best in that situation.

transcript.chinese: 不要让这种情况感觉，哦，我没有向他们展示我是谁。
transcript.english: Get your testosterone up.

transcript.chinese: 让这种情况感觉就像，我真的觉得我必须说出我是谁并展示我是谁。
transcript.english: Get your cortisol down.

transcript.chinese: 所以我想首先请你尝试摆姿势，我也想请你分享科学知识，因为这很简单。
transcript.english: Don't leave that situation feeling like, oh, I didn't show them who I am.

transcript.chinese: 我没有自我参与其中。 （笑声） 把它送给别人。
transcript.english: Leave that situation feeling like, I really feel like I got to say who I am and show who I am.

transcript.chinese: 与人分享，因为最能利用它的人是那些没有资源、没有技术、没有地位、没有权力的人。
transcript.english: So I want to ask you first, you know, both to try power posing, and also I want to ask you to share the science, because this is simple.

transcript.chinese: 把它交给他们，因为他们可以私下做。
transcript.english: I don't have ego involved in this. (Laughter) Give it away.

transcript.chinese: 他们需要自己的身体、隐私和两分钟，这可以显着改变他们的生活结果。
transcript.english: Share it with people, because the people who can use it the most are the ones with no resources and no technology and no status and no power.

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。
transcript.english: Give it to them because they can do it in private.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: They need their bodies, privacy and two minutes, and it can significantly change the outcomes of their life.

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。
transcript.english: Thank you.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
